evals.prompt: I need to run an unattended loop to fix all ESLint warnings in my project using Codex CLI. What's the safest way to set this up?
evals.expected_output: Classifies the work as iterative-pr, maps to Codex CLI adapter, generates starter commands with render_loop_command.py, and insists on max-runs and stop conditions.

evals.prompt: Help me resume my previous Claude Code session where I was refactoring the auth module.
evals.expected_output: Selects the resume pattern, generates Claude-specific resume commands, and advises using a notes file for cross-iteration state.

evals.prompt: I have a large RFC with multiple dependency layers. How do I orchestrate parallel execution across different parts?
evals.expected_output: Selects parallel-dag pattern, generates decomposition scaffolding, advises executing one ready layer at a time with merge review, and warns against full autopilot.
